libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Configure generated tests to run with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED`, and pass the same option to native-image build arguments. Exercise the library through its public Guice API (`Guice.createInjector`, `AbstractModule`, bindings, scopes, providers, interceptors) instead of copying old tests that import pre-2.9 internal classes such as `com.google.inject.internal.util.MapMaker` or unprefixed CGLIB classes; the 2.9.1 binary munges many internal implementation classes with leading `$` names.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Without the `--add-opens` option, basic injector construction on JDK 17+/25 can fail with `InaccessibleObjectException` from CGLIB/ReflectUtils while trying to access `ClassLoader#defineClass`.
The existing 2.1.7 tests are not mechanically reusable: several internal class imports no longer match the 2.9.1 artifact layout, which is the cause of the javac failure.

The artifact's required compile dependencies (`javax.inject:javax.inject:1` and `aopalliance:aopalliance:1.0`) are already transitive from `org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1`; optional SLF4J is not required for normal coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: sisu-guice 2.9.1 needs JVM/native-image access to java.lang for its bundled CGLIB bytecode generation on modern JDKs; no extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed.
